messages.content: You must return JSON only. No markdown, no code fences, no commentary. Return a single JSON object with keys: mode, topic, opinion, reasoning_summary.

Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

response_raw:
{
  "mode": "human",
  "topic": "How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.",
  "opinion": "As a 43-year-old carpenter living in Bari, I approach discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines with a mix of practicality and empathy, shaped by my personal experiences and traits. Being an atheist, I rely on scientific evidence and public health guidelines rather than faith, which helps ground these conversations in facts. My moderate openness means I'm willing to listen to different viewpoints, but my high agreeableness, scoring 92, drives me to seek harmony and avoid conflict, especially in sensitive topics like vaccines. Given my low extraversion, I prefer one-on-one discussions or small groups rather than large debates, which can feel overwhelming. My high neuroticism, at 88, makes me prone to anxiety, so I often feel stressed when these discussions turn heated or confrontational, as I worry about offending others or being misunderstood. To manage this, I focus on active listening and sharing my perspective calmly, drawing on my experience as a parent of two children, where I've had to make healthcare decisions based on safety and community well-being. Volunteering has taught me the importance of supporting others, so I emphasize compassion in these talks, acknowledging that people may have fears or misinformation. I avoid social media debates due to my limited time online, preferring face-to-face interactions where I can read emotions better. My hobbies like birdwatching and painting remind me of the value of patience and observation, which I apply by not rushing to judgment and considering all sides. Ultimately, I believe in promoting vaccination as a tool for public health, but I do so with kindness and respect, aiming to build trust rather than win arguments, which aligns with my desire to maintain relationships and contribute positively to my community.",
  "reasoning_summary": "Derived opinion from persona traits: Age 43 suggests maturity; occupation as carpenter implies hands-on, practical approach; atheism leads to science-based views; high agreeableness (92) prioritizes harmony and empathy; low extraversion (18) favors small-scale discussions; high neuroticism (88) causes anxiety in conflicts, prompting stress management; moderate openness (40) allows for listening but not radical shifts; hobbies like volunteering influence compassionate tone; parent of two relates to healthcare decisions; limited social media use avoids online debates; overall, tone is calm, empathetic, and fact-oriented to align with persona's background.",
  "tone": "calm, empathetic, practical, and slightly anxious",
  "citations": ["none"],
  "safety_notes": "none",

}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion: As a 43-year-old carpenter living in Bari, I approach discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines with a mix of practicality and empathy, shaped by my personal experiences and traits. Being an atheist, I rely on scientific evidence and public health guidelines rather than faith, which helps ground these conversations in facts. My moderate openness means I'm willing to listen to different viewpoints, but my high agreeableness, scoring 92, drives me to seek harmony and avoid conflict, especially in sensitive topics like vaccines. Given my low extraversion, I prefer one-on-one discussions or small groups rather than large debates, which can feel overwhelming. My high neuroticism, at 88, makes me prone to anxiety, so I often feel stressed when these discussions turn heated or confrontational, as I worry about offending others or being misunderstood. To manage this, I focus on active listening and sharing my perspective calmly, drawing on my experience as a parent of two children, where I've had to make healthcare decisions based on safety and community well-being. Volunteering has taught me the importance of supporting others, so I emphasize compassion in these talks, acknowledging that people may have fears or misinformation. I avoid social media debates due to my limited time online, preferring face-to-face interactions where I can read emotions better. My hobbies like birdwatching and painting remind me of the value of patience and observation, which I apply by not rushing to judgment and considering all sides. Ultimately, I believe in promoting vaccination as a tool for public health, but I do so with kindness and respect, aiming to build trust rather than win arguments, which aligns with my desire to maintain relationships and contribute positively to my community.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Derived opinion from persona traits: Age 43 suggests maturity; occupation as carpenter implies hands-on, practical approach; atheism leads to science-based views; high agreeableness (92) prioritizes harmony and empathy; low extraversion (18) favors small-scale discussions; high neuroticism (88) causes anxiety in conflicts, prompting stress management; moderate openness (40) allows for listening but not radical shifts; hobbies like volunteering influence compassionate tone; parent of two relates to healthcare decisions; limited social media use avoids online debates; overall, tone is calm, empathetic, and fact-oriented to align with persona's background.
response_parsed.tone: calm, empathetic, practical, and slightly anxious
